instruction: In Mail's settings, create a plain-text email signature named {SIGNAME} whose content is exactly: {SIGTEXT}
source: adapted:osworld:3f28fe4f-5d9d-4994-a456-efd78cfae1a3 mode=intent; OSWorld Thunderbird 'set up a plain text signature for my email account' — Thunderbird is NOT installed on macOS; the INTENT (define a named plain-text mail signature) is re-expressed against Apple Mail, whose signature content is read back via the Tier-C AppleScript one-shot

args: osascript -e 'tell application "Mail" to delete (every signature whose name is "{SIGNAME}")' 2>/dev/null || true
osascript -e 'tell application "Mail" to make new signature with properties {name:"{SIGNAME}", content:"{SIGTEXT}"}' 2>/dev/null || true

result.script: tell application "Mail" to return content of first signature whose name is "{SIGNAME}"
